name: Prisma Client Python (Type-Safe ORM)

auth.notes: DATABASE_URL env var; Prisma handles connection pooling

performance.notes: Rust-based Prisma engine; connection pooling; query caching possible

agent_readiness.notes: Type-safe Prisma ORM Python client. Generated from Prisma schema. schema.prisma: model User { id Int @id; name String; email String @unique }. prisma generate. from prisma import Prisma. db = Prisma(). await db.connect(). user = await db.user.find_unique(where={'email': 'a@b.com'}). users = await db.user.find_many(where={'name': {'contains': 'Alice'}}, order={'name': 'asc'}). new_user = await db.user.create(data={'name': 'Bob', 'email': 'b@b.com'}). await db.user.update(where={'id': 1}, data={'name': 'New'}). await db.user.delete(where={'id': 1}). Transactions. Relations. Raw SQL. Full type inference from schema.

gotchas: Requires Prisma CLI and `prisma generate` to regenerate client from schema
Prisma engine (Rust binary) downloaded on first use — ~20MB
Schema changes require `prisma migrate dev` and `prisma generate`
Not as battle-tested as SQLAlchemy for complex queries
AsyncIO only — no sync client without workarounds
best_when: FastAPI/asyncio apps requiring type-safe ORM with auto-completion
When Prisma schema is shared between Python and TypeScript backends
Modern async Python apps where SQLAlchemy async feels verbose
Rapid development with strong IDE autocomplete from generated types
avoid_when: Sync Python apps (SQLAlchemy or Peewee are better)
summary: Prisma Client Python provides type-safe async ORM generated from Prisma schema. await db.user.find_unique(where={'email': 'a@b.com'}). find_many(), create(), update(), delete(). Transactions. Relations. Full type inference. Apache-2.0 by Robert Craigie. Best for FastAPI apps sharing Prisma schema with TypeScript. Requires prisma generate on schema changes. Rust engine ~20MB download. Async only.
